name: Instagram: DM Send
summary: Send a single direct message to one Instagram handle.
description: Send one direct message to one Instagram handle. Opens the New Message modal, resolves the recipient via the typeahead search, selects them, opens the thread, types the message, and presses Enter to send. Each call creates a new message in the thread, so callers are responsible for deduping. Do not use this for multi-recipient broadcasts or replies to a specific earlier message — both need different entry points.

message_text.description: Text body of the message. Typed into the thread composer. Instagram enforces a 1000-character limit per message; this workflow rejects longer strings.

recipient_handle.description: Instagram handle of the recipient, without the leading @. Case-insensitive; the workflow normalizes to lowercase before matching typeahead rows.

steps.name: Open the New Message dialog and resolve the recipient

help.summary: Send a single direct message to one Instagram handle.

parameters.message_text: Text body of the message. Typed into the thread composer. Instagram enforces a 1000-character limit per message; this workflow rejects longer strings.
parameters.recipient_handle: Instagram handle of the recipient, without the leading @. Case-insensitive; the workflow normalizes to lowercase before matching typeahead rows.

examples.description: Send one DM to a public account
examples.command: rzn-browser run instagram dm-send --param recipient_handle="timbersfc" --param message_text="Hi! Big fan of the club."

returns: JSON with recipient_handle, message_text_preview (first 80 chars), and status: one of sent | login_required | recipient_not_found | select_failed | chat_button_disabled | thread_not_opened | composer_not_found | send_failed | over_length. On sent, also returns thread_url.
notes: Requires a logged-in Instagram session in the active Chrome profile; returns status=login_required if signed out.
Opens a dedicated workflow tab (use_current_tab=false), so this workflow is safe to run in parallel with other dedicated-tab instagram flows.
Not idempotent: every call sends a new message. Dedupe in the caller if needed.
Typeahead matching requires an exact handle in the option row — fuzzy matches on display name alone are intentionally rejected to avoid messaging the wrong person.

The thread composer is a ProseMirror-style contenteditable; this workflow uses document.execCommand('insertText', false, text) which is the only reliable path to trip Instagram's send-button enable logic.
Rate limiting is an Instagram-side concern: don't loop faster than a real user would. Space batched DMs with a real delay between calls.
